tipRules.tip: KVD: 'In stained water over grass during prespawn, the Red Eye Shad or slow-rolled spinnerbait are my go-to power baits. They cover water and feature inherent reaction qualities to trigger cold-water strikes.'
KVD: 'In clear cold water, the jerkbait may be a better choice than lipless or spinnerbait. You can alter its action by jerking, ripping, or pausing to interest neutral fish.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'I power fish year-round, even in cold water. Bass are far more active in cold water than I once believed. The strike zone is smaller but they'll crush a power bait worked in their immediate area. Power fishing is the foundation of everything I do — it helps locate fish and dial in patterns.'

KVD: 'In cold water, boat position is as critical as lure selection. Position parallel to dropoffs and edges — if you don't fish parallel, your bait catches a much smaller percentage of the key depth zone. Fish are less likely to move far.'

KVD: 'Cold-water bass respond to weather changes like in warmer seasons. Strike zone is largest on cloudy, rainy frontal days with wind — move lures faster. On high-pressure/no-wind days, slow down to bottom-bouncing baits but still fish them faster and erratically.'

KVD: '2008 Classic at Hartwell — brutal cold Day 1, hammered them cranking a weighted-to-suspend Series 5 over 15-25ft with stop-and-start retrieve. Day 2, post-frontal sunny skies killed that bite. Ran to the upper end with stained water and fished flat-sided cranks shallower — caught 12lbs in 2 hours.'
